prompt: Transfer openings in fire walls are:

options.text: Encouraged to improve tenant circulation
Generally prohibited or severely restricted because they compromise the wall's confinement function
Required at every floor for HVAC balance
Unrestricted when under 100 ft²

explanation: Fire walls create separate buildings; openings are limited and must meet stringent protectives when allowed.

distractorExplanations.a: Circulation convenience does not override fire wall integrity.
distractorExplanations.c: HVAC balance does not mandate fire wall openings.
distractorExplanations.d: Size does not eliminate restrictions on fire wall openings.

prompt: A fire window assembly differs from a fire door in that it:

options.text: Operates only as a smoke detector mounting panel
Requires no rating when glass is tempered only
Is a rated glazing and frame assembly tested to limit fire passage through window openings in rated walls
Replaces the need for exterior weather louvers

explanation: Fire-rated glazing assemblies (wired glass historically, now ceramic/glass units) are tested opening protectives.

distractorExplanations.a: Detector mounting is unrelated to fire window assemblies.
distractorExplanations.b: Weather louvers are separate from fire-rated glazing.
distractorExplanations.d: Weather louvers are separate from fire-rated glazing.

prompt: Rolling steel fire shutters are classified as:

options.text: Permanent ventilation grilles needing no maintenance
Decorative ceiling coffers
Unrated loading dock bumpers
Opening protectives that close to shield large openings in rated walls when activated by fire

explanation: Rolling shutters are tested assemblies for storefronts, counters, and large wall openings in rated separations.

distractorExplanations.a: Ventilation grilles without listing are not fire shutters.
distractorExplanations.b: Ceiling coffers are architectural, not opening protectives.
distractorExplanations.c: Ceiling coffers are architectural, not opening protectives.

prompt: When a fire-rated floor assembly is penetrated by a plumbing stack, the penetration must be:

options.text: Firestopped with a listed system matching the floor rating and penetrating item type
Left open to allow drain venting of fire gases
Wrapped with fiberglass batt only for sound
Sealed with expandable foam not listed for fire

explanation: Floor penetrations require tested firestop systems; generic foam without listing does not restore rating.

distractorExplanations.b: Fiberglass batts are not listed firestop systems.
distractorExplanations.c: Fiberglass batts are not listed firestop systems.
distractorExplanations.d: Non-listed foam fails in fire exposure.

prompt: Annular space at a penetration is:

options.text: The total room area divided by occupant load
The gap between the penetrating item and the edge of the opening in the rated assembly
The stair width minus handrail projection
The duct velocity pressure in inches w.c.

explanation: Firestop design addresses annular space fill material and size limits per the listed system.

distractorExplanations.a: Stair width is an egress calculation.
distractorExplanations.c: Stair width is an egress calculation.
distractorExplanations.d: Duct velocity is an HVAC parameter.

prompt: NFPA 221 is titled and scoped to address:

options.text: Sprinkler system hydraulic calculation procedures
Portable fire extinguisher hydrostatic testing
Standard for High Challenge Fire Walls, Fire Walls, and Fire Barrier Walls
Wildland urban interface defensible space only

explanation: NFPA 221 defines types of fire walls and fire barrier walls and their construction features for confining fire.

distractorExplanations.a: Sprinkler hydraulics are covered in NFPA 13.
distractorExplanations.b: WUI defensible space is addressed in other NFPA documents.
distractorExplanations.d: WUI defensible space is addressed in other NFPA documents.

prompt: A high challenge fire wall under NFPA 221 is used where:

options.text: Single-family residential detached garages only
Open parking structures with no enclosed areas
Temporary tents for farmers markets
Occupancies with very high fuel loads or hazards require enhanced wall performance beyond standard fire walls

explanation: High challenge fire walls address severe hazard occupancies with stringent construction requirements.

distractorExplanations.a: Detached garages typically use standard separations, not high challenge walls.
distractorExplanations.b: Open parking has different protection schemes.
distractorExplanations.c: Open parking has different protection schemes.

prompt: Fire-resistance rating is determined by:

options.text: Standardized fire tests (e.g., ASTM E119) measuring time until failure criteria are reached for walls, floors, and assemblies
Architect's specification note without testing
Paint color and wallpaper selection
The age of the building multiplied by two

explanation: Ratings (20 min, 1 hr, 2 hr, etc.) come from listed test results of complete assemblies.

distractorExplanations.b: Finishes do not establish fire resistance.
distractorExplanations.c: Finishes do not establish fire resistance.
distractorExplanations.d: Building age is not a rating metric.

prompt: A ceiling membrane of a fire-resistance-rated floor-ceiling assembly contributes to:

options.text: Only acoustic performance with no fire role
The assembly rating when tested as part of the floor-ceiling system protecting the space below
Exterior wind load resistance on curtain wall
Soil bearing capacity at the foundation

explanation: The ceiling, joists, decking, and protection materials act together as a horizontal fire barrier.

distractorExplanations.a: Ceiling membranes are integral to floor-ceiling fire ratings.
distractorExplanations.c: Curtain wall wind loads are unrelated.
distractorExplanations.d: Foundation bearing is structural geotechnical design.

prompt: Fireblocking differs from firestopping in that fireblocking:

options.text: Is a listed penetration seal system tested to ASTM E814 only
Applies exclusively to exterior window mullions
Uses building materials to obstruct concealed draft openings at intersections and concealed spaces at prescribed intervals
Replaces fire dampers in all duct shafts

explanation: Fireblocking limits hidden fire spread in cavities; firestopping restores ratings at through-penetrations.

distractorExplanations.a: ASTM E814 systems are firestopping, not fireblocking.
distractorExplanations.b: Dampers and fireblocking serve different purposes.
distractorExplanations.d: Dampers and fireblocking serve different purposes.

prompt: In a fully sprinklered building, some codes permit:

options.text: Complete removal of all fire walls without engineering analysis
Elimination of every fire damper regardless of penetration
Use of unrated hollow-core doors at all shaft openings
Reduction of certain fire barrier ratings when sprinklers are installed per qualifying criteria

explanation: Sprinkler trade-ups are code-specific; they reduce ratings in defined cases but do not eliminate all passive protection.

distractorExplanations.a: Fire walls are rarely removed solely due to sprinklers.
distractorExplanations.b: Damper requirements often remain at rated penetrations.
distractorExplanations.c: Damper requirements often remain at rated penetrations.

prompt: A tenant separation wall in a multi-tenant mercantile building is typically a:

options.text: Fire partition or fire barrier rated per occupancy and area provisions extending to the underside of the deck above
Nonrated stud wall with only insulation batts
Exterior limestone veneer
Temporary barricade during renovation only

explanation: Demising walls between tenants must meet fire separation requirements based on occupancy, size, and construction type.

distractorExplanations.b: Exterior veneer is not an interior demising partition.
distractorExplanations.c: Exterior veneer is not an interior demising partition.
distractorExplanations.d: Temporary barricades are not permanent separations.

prompt: Dwelling unit separations in apartment buildings commonly require:

options.text: No separation when buildings are under four stories
Fire partitions (often 1-hour) between units and between units and corridors per building code tables
Only smoke detectors without any rated walls
Open mezzanines between all units

explanation: Fire partitions limit unit-to-unit spread; ratings depend on construction type, height, and sprinkler status.

distractorExplanations.a: Low-rise does not automatically eliminate separations.
distractorExplanations.c: Detection supplements but does not replace rated separations.
distractorExplanations.d: Open mezzanines between units would violate separation.

prompt: Corridor walls in many occupancies are required to be:

options.text: Unrated glass partitions for visibility only
Chain-link fencing for security
Fire partitions or fire barriers with rated doors limiting smoke and fire spread along the egress path
Operable fabric banners

explanation: Corridor ratings protect egress routes; door ratings pair with wall ratings per code tables.

distractorExplanations.a: Unrated glass may not meet corridor wall requirements.
distractorExplanations.b: Fabric banners provide no fire resistance.
distractorExplanations.d: Fabric banners provide no fire resistance.

prompt: A fire door with a failing latch or missing closer is considered:

options.text: Acceptable if the door looks visually aligned
Irrelevant when the building has a fire pump
Compliant if propped open only during lunch
A significant impairment to the fire confinement strategy because the opening protective cannot perform as designed

explanation: NFPA 80 requires functional closers, latches, and intact labels; impaired doors compromise compartmentation.

distractorExplanations.a: Visual alignment does not ensure self-closing and latching.
distractorExplanations.b: Fire pumps do not compensate for open or unlatched fire doors.
distractorExplanations.c: Fire pumps do not compensate for open or unlatched fire doors.

prompt: Gasketing on fire door assemblies is used to:

options.text: Limit smoke passage and assist intumescent seals in maintaining the door's performance in a fire
Decorate the door with color accents
Increase door weight for security only
Eliminate the need for a door frame

explanation: Smoke seals and intumescent gasketing are part of listed fire door assemblies where required.

distractorExplanations.b: Weight alone does not establish fire performance.
distractorExplanations.c: Weight alone does not establish fire performance.
distractorExplanations.d: Frames are required components of door assemblies.

prompt: An elevator hoistway enclosure must resist:

options.text: Only water leakage from roof drains
Fire and smoke spread through the vertical shaft connecting multiple floors
Seismic soil liquefaction at the site
Wind uplift on the parapet exclusively

explanation: Hoistway enclosures are shaft enclosures with rated walls and protected landing openings.

distractorExplanations.a: Water leakage is a weatherproofing issue, not the primary hoistway function.
distractorExplanations.d: Parapet wind uplift is unrelated to hoistway fire confinement.

prompt: Penthouse structures above a roof require fire wall or barrier consideration because:

options.text: Penthouses are always outside the building and need no separation
Roof color determines fire rating
They can allow fire spread from the penthouse into the main building or between separated sections
Mechanical equipment never ignites

explanation: Continuity of rated assemblies at roof transitions prevents bypass of compartmentation.

distractorExplanations.a: Penthouses connected to the building need rated separations as required.
distractorExplanations.b: Mechanical equipment can be an ignition source.
distractorExplanations.d: Mechanical equipment can be an ignition source.

prompt: A fire-rated joint system is used at:

options.text: Decorative crown molding in lobbies only
Exterior landscape edging at grade
Parking stripe layout in garages
Interfaces between adjacent fire-rated assemblies that may move independently (e.g., floor-to-wall joints)

explanation: Joint systems (tested per ASTM E1966 / UL 2079) maintain rating at dynamic joints.

distractorExplanations.a: Crown molding is not a rated joint system.

prompt: Perimeter fire containment systems at floor edges in curtain wall buildings address:

options.text: The gap between the floor slab edge and the curtain wall to prevent floor-to-floor fire spread
Interior carpet tile adhesive selection
Desk layout in open offices
Coffee service in break rooms

explanation: Perimeter firestop/containment restores the horizontal assembly at the slab perimeter — a common vulnerability.

prompt: A smoke damper (without fire damper function) may be permitted when:

options.text: Any duct penetrates a 2-hour fire wall without exception
The penetration is only through a smoke barrier and the code requires smoke leakage control without a fire rating at that opening
The duct is in an unlisted grease exhaust plenum at a rated shaft
All HVAC systems in high-rise buildings universally

explanation: Smoke dampers address leakage at smoke barriers; fire or combination dampers are needed at fire-rated penetrations.

distractorExplanations.a: Fire walls require fire-rated protection at duct penetrations.
distractorExplanations.c: Grease ducts have specific protection rules beyond smoke dampers alone.
distractorExplanations.d: High-rise rules still distinguish smoke vs fire-rated locations.

prompt: Inspection of fire dampers after installation should verify:

options.text: Only the paint color of the duct exterior
That the damper is wired open for maximum airflow
Accessibility, correct orientation, fusible link rating, and unobstructed closure in the rated assembly
Removal of all access panels to hide the damper

explanation: Fire dampers must be accessible for inspection per NFPA 80/90 and building codes; wired open is prohibited.

distractorExplanations.a: Duct paint is irrelevant to damper function.
distractorExplanations.b: Hiding dampers prevents required inspection and maintenance.
distractorExplanations.d: Hiding dampers prevents required inspection and maintenance.

prompt: An opening in a fire barrier for a duct air transfer grille (pass-through) typically requires:

options.text: Nothing if the grille is decorative aluminum
Only a carbon monoxide detector nearby
Removal of the fire barrier in that bay
A listed fire damper or other approved opening protective unless a specific code exception applies

explanation: Air transfer openings can convey fire and smoke; protectives or exceptions govern their use in rated walls.

distractorExplanations.a: Decorative grilles without listing do not protect openings.
distractorExplanations.b: CO detectors do not block fire passage.
distractorExplanations.c: CO detectors do not block fire passage.

prompt: The primary purpose of limiting compartment size through fire barriers is to:

options.text: Keep fire size manageable for sprinkler design, egress time, and fire department intervention
Maximize open floor plate for furniture vendors
Increase smoke spread for early detector activation only
Eliminate all interior walls for aesthetics

explanation: Compartment area limits align hazard growth with available egress and suppression response.

distractorExplanations.b: Smoke spread is limited, not encouraged.
distractorExplanations.c: Smoke spread is limited, not encouraged.
distractorExplanations.d: Aesthetics do not override compartmentation requirements.

prompt: When evaluating a building's confining fire features, the CFPS professional should prioritize verifying:

options.text: Only the exterior landscaping irrigation schedule
Continuity of rated assemblies, proper opening protectives, and listed firestopping at penetrations
Employee uniform colors in the lobby
Social media postings about fire drills

explanation: Passive confinement depends on continuous rated construction and maintained opening protectives and firestopping.

distractorExplanations.a: Irrigation is unrelated to fire confinement.
distractorExplanations.d: Social media is not a field verification item.
